questions.question: Which of the following methods can be used to obtain pure water from a mixture of sand, water and methanoic acid?
options: neutralization with NaOH followed by filtration
neutralization with NaOH followed by distillation
questions.explanation: Sand is separated by filtration, then distillation separates water from methanoic acid based on different boiling points.

questions.question: How many atoms are present in 6.0g of magnesium? [Mg = 24, NA = 6.02 x 10²³ mol⁻¹]
50 cm³ of gas was collected over water at 10°C and 765 mm Hg. Calculate the volume of the gas at s.t.p. if the saturated vapour pressure of water at 10°C is 5mm Hg
questions.explanation: Apply combined gas law accounting for water vapor pressure: (765-5)/760 × 50/22.4 × 22.4 = 48.23 cm³

questions.question: An increase in the pressure exerted on gas at a constant temperature results in
options: a decrease in the number of effective collisions
an increase in the average intermolecular distance
questions.explanation: Boyle's Law states that at constant temperature, pressure and volume are inversely proportional.

questions.question: 2H₂(g) + O₂(g) → 2H₂O(g). In the reaction above, what volume of hydrogen would be left over when 300 cm³ of oxygen and 1000 cm³ of hydrogen are exploded in a sealed tube?
questions.explanation: From stoichiometry, 300 cm³ O₂ requires 600 cm³ H₂. Excess H₂ = 1000 - 600 = 400 cm³

questions.question: Which of the following can correctly be listed as evidences for the particulate nature of matter? I. Evaporation II. Sublimation III. Diffusion IV. Brownian motion
questions.explanation: All four phenomena demonstrate particle behavior: evaporation (escape), sublimation (direct transition), diffusion (particle mixing), and Brownian motion (random particle movement).

questions.question: If the elements X and Y have atomic numbers 11 and 17 respectively, what type of bond can they form?
questions.explanation: X (Na, Z=11) is a metal and Y (Cl, Z=17) is a non-metal. They form an ionic bond (NaCl).

questions.question: A hydrogen atom which has lost an electron contains
options: one proton, one electron and one neutron
questions.explanation: A hydrogen atom has 1 proton and 1 electron. When it loses the electron (H⁺), only the proton remains.

questions.question: The electronic configuration of Mg²⁺ is
questions.explanation: Mg (Z=12) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s². Mg²⁺ loses 2 electrons from 3s, giving 1s² 2s² 2p⁶.

questions.question: Group VII elements are
questions.explanation: Group VII (halogens) are highly electronegative with 7 valence electrons, making them strong oxidizing agents that readily gain electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to study the arrangement of particles in crystal lattices?
questions.explanation: X-rays have wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing and are diffracted by crystal structures (X-ray crystallography).

questions.question: The chemicals used to soften hard water involves the addition of
options: insoluble sodium compounds which form soluble solutions of calcium and magnesium
soluble sodium compounds which form soluble solutions of calcium and magnesium ions
soluble sodium compounds which form insoluble precipitates of calcium and magnesium ions
insoluble precipitates of calcium and magnesium ions
questions.explanation: Water softening uses soluble sodium compounds like Na₂CO₃ to precipitate Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions as insoluble carbonates.

questions.question: Chlorination of water for town supply is carried out to
questions.explanation: Chlorine (Cl₂) is a strong disinfectant that kills bacteria, viruses, and other microorganisms in water.

questions.question: The solubilities of different solutes in a given solvent can be compared by
options: plotting their solubility curves on separate axes
plotting their solubility curves on the same axes
plotting some of the solubility curves on the x-axis and others on the y-axis
plotting their solubility curves on the x-axis only
questions.explanation: Plotting on the same axes allows direct visual comparison of solubility trends across different solutes.

questions.question: Potassium trioxochlorate (V) has a solubility of 1.5 moldm⁻³ at 45°C. On cooling this solution to 20°C, the solubility was found to be 0.5 mol dm⁻³. What mass of KClO₃ was crystallized out? [K = 39, Cl = 35.5, O =16]
questions.explanation: Molar mass of KClO₃ = 122.5 g/mol. Decrease in molarity = 1.5 - 0.5 = 1.0 mol. Mass = 1.0 × 122.5 = 122.50g

questions.question: Which of the following pollutants is associated with brain damage?
questions.explanation: Radioactive fallout contains isotopes that accumulate in tissues and damage the nervous system and brain.

questions.question: Which of the following will produce a solution with pH less than 7 at equivalent point?
questions.explanation: HCl (strong acid) + Mg(OH)₂ (weak base) produces acidic salt MgCl₂, resulting in pH < 7 at equivalence point.

questions.question: The number of hydroxonium ions produced by one molecule of an acid in aqueous solution is its
questions.explanation: Basicity is the number of replaceable hydrogen ions (H⁺/H₃O⁺) an acid can donate per molecule.

questions.question: During a titration experiment, 0.05 moles of carbon (IV) oxide is liberated. What is the volume of gas liberated?
questions.explanation: At STP, 1 mole of gas = 22.4 dm³. Volume = 0.05 × 22.4 = 1.12 dm³

questions.question: A major factor considered in selecting a suitable method for preparing a simple salt is its
questions.explanation: Solubility determines if the salt can be crystallized from solution and separated from other products.

questions.question: The oxidation number of boron in NaBH₄ is
questions.explanation: In NaBH₄: Na is +1, H is -1. So B + 4(-1) = -1 - 1, giving B = +3. However, B is actually -3 in this hydride.

questions.question: 2Na₂O₂(s) + 2H₂O(l) → 4NaOH(s) + O₂(s). The substance that is oxidized in the reaction above is
questions.explanation: In Na₂O₂, oxygen has -1 oxidation state. It is oxidized to 0 in O₂ and reduced to -2 in OH⁻.

questions.question: What number of moles of Cu²⁺ will be deposited by 360 coulombs of electricity? [F = 96500 C mol⁻¹]
A metal M displaces zinc from ZnCl₂ solution. This shows that
options: electrons flow from zinc to M
M is more electropositive than zinc
M is more electronegative than zinc
zinc is more electropositive than M
questions.explanation: If M displaces Zn, M is more reactive and loses electrons more easily, hence more electropositive.

questions.question: CO(g) + H₂O(g) → CO₂(g) + H₂(g). Calculate the standard heat change of the reaction above, if the standard enthalpies of formation of CO₂(g), H₂O(g) and CO(g) in KJ mol⁻¹ are -394, -242 and -110 respectively.
An increase in entropy can best be illustrated by
questions.explanation: Mixing gases increases disorder and entropy. Freezing, condensation, and solidification decrease entropy.

questions.question: The highest rate of production of carbon (IV) oxide can be achieved using
questions.explanation: Higher molarity (0.10) increases concentration, and powdered form increases surface area for faster reaction.

questions.question: 2CO(g) + O₂(g) ⇋ 2CO₂(g). In the reaction above, high pressure will favour the forward reaction because
options: the reaction is in dynamic equilibrium
the process occurs with a decrease in volume
questions.explanation: Le Chatelier's principle: 3 moles gas → 2 moles. High pressure shifts equilibrium toward fewer moles (forward).

questions.question: A piece of filter paper moistened with lead (II) ethanoate solution turns black when the paper is dropped into a gas likely to be
questions.explanation: H₂S forms black PbS precipitate with Pb²⁺ ions from lead ethanoate.

questions.question: Which of the following gases has a characteristic pungent smell, turns red litmus paper blue and forms dense white fumes with hydrogen chloride gas?
questions.explanation: NH₃ is pungent, basic (turns litmus blue), and forms dense white NH₄Cl fumes with HCl gas.

questions.question: Commercial bleaching can be carried out using
questions.explanation: Cl₂ is an oxidative bleach and SO₂ is a reductive bleach; both are used in commercial bleaching.

questions.question: Mineral acids are usually added to commercial hydrogen peroxide to
options: reduce it to water and oxygen
questions.explanation: Acids are added as stabilizers to prevent decomposition of H₂O₂ during storage.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds will burn with a brick-red colour in a non-luminous Bunsen flame?
questions.explanation: Calcium compounds produce a characteristic brick-red flame due to electron transitions in Ca atoms.

questions.question: The purest form of iron which contains only about 0.1% carbon is
questions.explanation: Wrought iron is commercially pure iron with minimal carbon content, making it malleable and ductile.

questions.question: A common characteristic between zinc and the other transition elements is the ability to
questions.explanation: Transition elements and zinc readily form complex ions due to unfilled d-orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following metals is the least reactive?
questions.explanation: Gold (Au) is the least reactive due to its filled electron shells and high reduction potential.

questions.question: Geometric isomerism can exist in
questions.explanation: Hex-3-ene has alkene groups allowing cis-trans isomerism. Others lack suitable double bonds or substituents.

questions.question: Alkanals can be distinguished from alkanones by the reaction with
questions.explanation: Alkanals are oxidized by Fehling's reagent to carboxylic acids (positive test), while alkanones are not oxidized.

questions.question: The isomers of C₃H₈O are
questions.explanation: C₃H₈O has two structural isomers: 1-propanol (primary alcohol) and 2-propanol (secondary alcohol).

questions.question: Carbohydrates are large molecules with the molecular formula Cₓ(H₂O)ᵧ. In which of the following pairs is x not equal to y?
questions.explanation: Glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆, x=6, y=6) and starch (C₆H₁₀O₅, x=6, y=5) have x ≠ y in starch.

questions.question: A compound contains 40.0% C, 6.7% H, 53.3% O. If the molecular mass of the compound is 180, its molecular formula is [C =12, H =1, O=16]
questions.explanation: Empirical formula: C:H:O = 40/12 : 6.7/1 : 53.3/16 = 1:2:1 (CH₂O). Molecular formula: (CH₂O)n where n = 180/30 = 6, giving C₆H₁₂O₆.

questions.question: The alkyne that will give a white precipitate with silver trioxonitrate (V) is
questions.explanation: Terminal alkynes (C≡C-H) form white silver acetylide precipitates with Ag⁺. Only option 2 has a terminal alkyne.

questions.question: The saponification of an alkanoate to produce soap and alkanol involves
questions.explanation: Saponification is the hydrolysis of esters using alkali, breaking the ester bond to form soap (salt) and alcohol.

questions.question: 2-methyl propan-2-ol is an example of a
questions.explanation: In (CH₃)₃C-OH, the OH group is attached to a carbon bonded to three other carbons, making it tertiary.

questions.question: The final oxidation product of alkanol, alkanal and alkanones is
questions.explanation: Both alkanols and alkanals oxidize to carboxylic acids (alkanoic acids). Alkanones cannot be oxidized further.

questions.question: Ethanol reacts with concentrated tetraoxosulphate (VI) acid at a temperature above 170°C to form
questions.explanation: Above 170°C, ethanol undergoes dehydration with concentrated H₂SO₄ to form ethene (C₂H₄).

questions.question: An example of oxidation-reduction enzyme is
questions.explanation: Dehydrogenases catalyze redox reactions by transferring hydrogen/electrons. Other enzymes are hydrolases.
